sources.notes: Answer parity queries on an odd array whose structure forbids alternating x,y,x,y subsequences.
Official solution constructs a tree from value intervals, maps array positions to a DFS order, and answers queries using LCA and parity masks.

annotation.summary: Answer range parity queries on an array with a forbidden alternation property by exploiting the induced tree structure of value intervals.
annotation.constraints: n and q are large, so queries need logarithmic tree preprocessing rather than scanning ranges.
annotation.core_idea: The condition that no two values alternate as x,y,x,y makes the occurrence intervals laminar: intervals are either disjoint or nested. This laminar family forms a rooted tree. Map each array position to the node/value interval containing it; a query over positions can be decomposed using the LCA of the corresponding tree nodes, and parity information is maintained with xor masks along root paths and ordered child boundaries.
annotation.complexity: O((n+q) log n) with binary-lifting LCA and ordered child searches.

solution_variants.name: laminar-tree-lca
solution_variants.summary: Construct the laminar interval tree of values, preprocess LCA, and answer parity queries by decomposing paths and boundary children.

tags.evidence: The array's forbidden alternation property is transformed into a laminar interval tree.
Queries are answered by taking the LCA of the two endpoint nodes in the constructed interval tree.
tags.description: Lowest common ancestor preprocessing and queries on rooted trees, including binary lifting or Euler-tour RMQ approaches.
tags.created_reason: LCA is a classic tree-query primitive and should be searchable independently from generic tree tags.

tags.evidence: Parity information over values is combined with xor-style masks along tree paths.
The key structural observation is that forbidding x,y,x,y creates nested/disjoint occurrence intervals.
